sections.paragraph:
pl. עִזִּים f.-

(1) a she-goat. (Syr. ܥܙܶܐܳ; Arab. عَنْزُ; Phœnic. ἄζα, Steph. Byz. The same word is found in the Indo-Germanic languages, as the Sansc. adsha, a he-goat; adsĥ, a she-goat; Goth. ǵitsa; Anglo-Sax. ĝt; Germ. Geis, with a harder form, Gems, chamois; Gr. αἴξ, αἰγός; also the Turkish gieik, ghieizi; comp. Grimm, Deutsche Gram. iii. 328.) גְּדִי עִזִּים a kid of goats, Gen 27:9. שֵׂה עִזִּים a goat, i.e. the goat (ein Stück Ziegenvieh), [an individual for the species], Deu 14:4.

(2) pl. עִזִּים goats’ hair, Exo 26:7 36:14 1Sa 19:13.

עז

Ch. i.q. Heb. No. 1, Ezr 6:17.
